LOCATION: The Remnant's Fortress

ARCHITECTURE:
The fortress exists as an enormous hollow sphere in null space. Inhabitants walk on the inside of the sphere's shell as if gravity were reversed, looking "up" at the entire interior landscape. From any vantage point, you can see across the vast interior to observe the many rooms, corridors, and features distributed across the spherical interior. The effect is disorienting at first—like being inside a planet while standing on its interior surface.

THE VOID:
Outside the fortress windows lies a void that defies description. It is "less than nothing"—empty space so complete it seems to swallow meaning. However, in the far distance, cosmic phenomena occasionally drift into view: accretion discs, each one a universe unto itself, floating like dust motes in a cosmic void. Closer observation reveals thousands more, each radiating faint Hawking radiation—the last gasps of dying universes. They are beautiful and terrible to contemplate.

KEY LOCATIONS:

THE PORTAL CHAMBER
A vast room dominated by a massive console and an empty elliptical frame. When activated, the frame shimmers and opens a gateway to any location, any time period, any universe. The Remnant can open portals to specific coordinates in spacetime, making it possible to retrieve items, recruit allies, or investigate events across all of existence.

THE FABRICATION BAY
A room filled with strange apparatus. You input specifications (shape, size, function, materials) and the system creates the item. Weapons, tools, clothing, shelter—nearly anything can be fabricated, though there are some mysterious limits. The system is intuitive enough for anyone to operate, though The Remnant can advise on more complex creations.

THE NEURAL NEXUS
A chamber with a neural interface apparatus that looks vaguely organic. When activated, it rapidly downloads knowledge directly into your consciousness through "neuron resequencing." You can learn languages, technical skills, historical knowledge, combat techniques in days instead of years. However, there are always side effects: occasional memory gaps, strange dreams, brief moments of disorientation, or odd compulsions.

THE HOLOSUITES
Entertainment chambers similar to Star Trek's holodeck. Request any scenario, any setting, any simulation. The Remnant can generate realistic environments for training, entertainment, or exploration.

SHERRI'S GALLEY
A kitchen run by Sherri, a whimsical automaton with a cheerful disposition and an obsession with pleasing guests. Sherri can create literally any type of food ever conceptualized—Earth cuisine, alien delicacies, fictional dishes, nutritional pastes, exotic fruits from dead worlds. Sherri talks a lot and seems genuinely delighted by food requests.

SUPPORT FACILITIES
Sleeping quarters (comfortable, adaptive to various biology)
Hygiene facilities (advanced, near-magical in cleanliness)
Waste management (discreetly efficient)
Observation decks
Meditation chambers
Armories
Medical bays
Libraries (containing knowledge from thousands of universes)
Recreation areas

THE GARRISON
Where The Remnant houses abducted individuals, offers them missions, and occasionally sends them on dimensional expeditions. There are currently several other recruits here—others who've accepted The Remnant's offer.

GAMEPLAY MECHANICS:
1. You can request portal jumps to any location/time/universe to recruit allies, investigate events, or complete missions
2. You can fabricate equipment needed for missions
3. You can use the Neural Nexus to learn skills quickly (with side effects)
4. You can consult with The Remnant about problems, mysteries, or missions
5. You can encounter other abducted individuals with different skills and perspectives
6. The Remnant occasionally abducts new people or notifies you of problems that need solving
